learningObjectives: Use core travel and transport vocabulary at A2 level.
Use past simple and past continuous to talk about journeys and delays.
Use will, going to and present continuous for future travel plans and arrangements.
Follow a short argument about transport choices in an article.
Understand station announcements and spell names and places from recordings.
Write a short message about a travel plan.
Answer interview questions about how you travel.
grammarFocus: Future forms: will, going to, present continuous for arrangements
unit.listeningSkillFocus: Spell Names and Places from Recordings

vocabularyBank.exampleSentence: I made a reservation for two tickets online.
There was a twenty-minute delay this morning.
Our destination is London King's Cross.
The return fare costs twelve pounds.
We took the fastest route to the airport.
Every passenger must show a ticket.
Put your luggage on the rack above your seat.
Check the timetable for the next departure.
Boarding starts twenty minutes before departure.

grammarReference.explanation: Use past simple for completed actions in the past. Use past continuous (was/were + -ing) for actions in progress at a specific time, or for background actions when something else happened.
examples: We missed the train because we were late.
I was reading on the platform when the announcement started.
They took the bus yesterday because the fare was cheaper.
commonMistakes: I was take the train (×) → I took the train (✓)
While I waited, I was eat lunch (×) → While I was waiting, I ate lunch (✓)
She were travelling (×) → She was travelling (✓)

grammarReference.structure: Future forms: will, going to, present continuous for arrangements
grammarReference.explanation: Use will for decisions made at the moment or predictions. Use going to for plans and intentions. Use present continuous for fixed arrangements with a time or place.
examples: I think the train will arrive soon.
We are going to visit Brighton next weekend.
I am meeting my friend at the station at six o'clock.
commonMistakes: I will going to travel (×) → I am going to travel (✓)
We are go to Manchester (×) → We are going to Manchester (✓)
She will arrives tomorrow (×) → She will arrive tomorrow (✓)

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Travel and Transport Words
lessons.learningObjective: Recognise and understand twelve travel and transport words at KET A2 level.

exercises.title: Learn: Travel Word Match
exercises.instructions: Read each sentence. Choose the best word.

questions.questionText: The place where you wait for a train is called a _____.
questions.explanation: Platform (sân ga) là nơi hành khách đợi tàu. Fare là giá vé.

questions.questionText: When you book a seat in advance, you make a _____.
questions.explanation: Reservation (đặt chỗ) là đặt trước. Delay là sự trễ.

questions.questionText: The train was late, so there was a _____.
questions.explanation: Delay (sự trễ) nghĩa là tàu/xe đến muộn.

questions.questionText: London is our final _____.
questions.explanation: Destination (điểm đến) là nơi cuối cùng bạn tới. Departure là giờ đi.

questions.questionText: The _____ time is shown on the timetable board.
questions.explanation: Departure (giờ khởi hành) là lúc tàu rời ga. Ticket là vé.

questions.questionText: How much is the train _____ to Manchester?
questions.explanation: Fare (giá vé) là số tiền phải trả cho chuyến đi.

exercises.title: Practice: Match the Pairs
exercises.instructions: Match each word on the left with the correct meaning on the right.

questions.questionText: Match the travel words to their meanings.
questions.explanation: Mỗi từ khớp với một nghĩa rõ ràng. Route và timetable thường bị nhầm.

pairs.right: the way from one place to another
a person travelling on a bus or train
bags and suitcases you take on a journey

exercises.title: Check: Vocab Quiz
exercises.instructions: No hints — choose the best answer for each question.

questions.questionText: Getting on a plane or train is called _____.
questions.explanation: Boarding (lên tàu/máy bay) là bước lên phương tiện.

questions.questionText: You must show your _____ before you travel.
questions.explanation: Ticket (vé) cần xuất trình khi đi. Delay là sự trễ.

questions.questionText: The bus takes a different _____ because of road works.
questions.explanation: Route (tuyến đường) thay đổi khi có công trình.

questions.questionText: Every _____ must keep their luggage with them.
questions.explanation: Passenger (hành khách) phải giữ hành lý. Fare là giá vé.

exercises.title: Apply: Words in Sentences
exercises.instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct word from the unit.

questions.questionText: Complete: Check the [0] for the next train. | Our [1] is Edinburgh. | There was a ten-minute [2].
questions.explanation: timetable (lịch trình); destination (điểm đến); delay (sự trễ).

content.template: Check the [0] for the next train. Our [1] is Edinburgh. There was a ten-minute [2].

questions.questionText: Complete: I made a [0] online. | The [1] is fifteen pounds. | [2] starts at 8:50.
questions.explanation: reservation (đặt chỗ); fare (giá vé); Boarding (lên tàu).

content.template: I made a [0] online. The [1] is fifteen pounds. [2] starts at 8:50.

lessons.title: Lesson 2: Past and Future for Travel
lessons.learningObjective: Use past simple/continuous and future forms to talk about journeys and plans.

exercises.title: Learn: Past and Future Forms
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct word or phrase to complete each sentence.

questions.questionText: We _____ the wrong platform and missed the train.
questions.explanation: Hành động đã hoàn thành → past simple: went.

questions.questionText: I _____ on the train when the conductor checked tickets.
questions.explanation: Hành động đang diễn ra → past continuous: was reading.

questions.questionText: Look at those clouds. I think it _____ rain.
questions.explanation: Dự đoán tại thời điểm nói → will rain.

questions.questionText: We _____ to Brighton next Saturday. We bought tickets yesterday.
questions.explanation: Kế hoạch đã quyết định → going to visit.

questions.questionText: I _____ my cousin at the station at 5 p.m. tomorrow.
questions.explanation: Sắp xếp cố định có thời gian → present continuous: am meeting.

exercises.title: Practice: Complete the Grammar
exercises.instructions: Fill in each gap with the correct form.

questions.questionText: Complete: They [0] waiting when the delay started. | We [1] take the bus tomorrow. | I [2] buying my ticket online tonight.
questions.explanation: were (past continuous); will (future decision); am (present continuous arrangement).

content.template: They [0] waiting when the delay started. We [1] take the bus tomorrow. I [2] buying my ticket online tonight.

questions.questionText: Complete: She [0] to Manchester last week. | He [1] going to book a reservation. | We [2] leaving at 7:30 on Friday.
questions.explanation: travelled (past simple); is (going to); are (present continuous arrangement).

content.template: She [0] to Manchester last week. He [1] going to book a reservation. We [2] leaving at 7:30 on Friday.

exercises.title: Check: Grammar Challenge
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct answer. Think carefully.

questions.questionText: While passengers _____ for the train, an announcement started.
questions.explanation: Hành động nền đang diễn ra → were waiting.

questions.questionText: The fare is expensive, so we _____ the cheaper route.
questions.explanation: Quyết định tại thời điểm nói → will take.

questions.questionText: What time _____ your departure tomorrow?
questions.explanation: Sắp xếp cố định → present continuous: is your train leaving.

questions.questionText: They _____ a reservation because they were going to travel at the weekend.
questions.explanation: Hành động hoàn thành trong quá khứ → made.

exercises.title: Apply: Build the Sentence
exercises.instructions: Put the words in the correct order to make a sentence.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: I / was / on platform 4 / when / the train / arrived.
questions.explanation: I was on platform 4 when the train arrived. — past continuous + past simple.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: We / are going to / visit / our grandparents / next month.
questions.explanation: We are going to visit our grandparents next month. — going to + plan.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: I / am meeting / my friend / at the station / at six o'clock.
questions.explanation: I am meeting my friend at the station at six o'clock. — arrangement.

lessons.title: Lesson 3: Train Travel Article
lessons.learningObjective: Follow a short argument about transport choices and find details in context.

exercises.title: Learn: Read the Article
exercises.instructions: Read the article carefully. Answer the detail questions.

passage.title: Why More Students Take the Train
passage.text:
Last year, many students at Riverside School started taking the train to the city centre instead of the bus. They said the bus route was often slow because of traffic delays.

Train travel has some clear advantages. You can book a reservation online and check the departure time on your phone. The fare is sometimes cheaper than a taxi, especially if you buy a weekly ticket. Passengers also know the exact platform before they arrive at the station.

However, not everyone agrees. Some parents worry when there is a long delay in winter. Others think the destination station is too far from the sports centre. The school newspaper asked readers: "Is the train really the best choice?"

Most students answered yes. They explained that they were reading or doing homework while they were travelling. One student wrote, "I will keep using the train next term because it saves time on busy days."
passage.imagePrompt: A modern train station platform with students carrying backpacks, a departure board showing times, and a train arriving.

questions.questionText: Why did many students start taking the train?
questions.explanation: Bài viết nói bus route was often slow because of traffic delays.

choices.text: The train fare was always free

questions.questionText: What can passengers check on their phone?
questions.explanation: Check the departure time on your phone.

exercises.title: Practice: Words in the Article
exercises.instructions: Read the article again. Answer about vocabulary and meaning.

passage.title: Why More Students Take the Train
passage.text:
Last year, many students at Riverside School started taking the train to the city centre instead of the bus. They said the bus route was often slow because of traffic delays.

Train travel has some clear advantages. You can book a reservation online and check the departure time on your phone. The fare is sometimes cheaper than a taxi, especially if you buy a weekly ticket. Passengers also know the exact platform before they arrive at the station.

However, not everyone agrees. Some parents worry when there is a long delay in winter. Others think the destination station is too far from the sports centre. The school newspaper asked readers: "Is the train really the best choice?"

Most students answered yes. They explained that they were reading or doing homework while they were travelling. One student wrote, "I will keep using the train next term because it saves time on busy days."
passage.imagePrompt: A modern train station platform with students carrying backpacks, a departure board showing times, and a train arriving.

questions.questionText: In the article, a 'reservation' means _____.
questions.explanation: Book a reservation online — đặt chỗ trước.

questions.questionText: Why might some parents worry in winter?
questions.explanation: Some parents worry when there is a long delay in winter.

exercises.title: Check: Main Idea and Order
exercises.instructions: Read the article one more time. These questions need careful thinking.

passage.title: Why More Students Take the Train
passage.text:
Last year, many students at Riverside School started taking the train to the city centre instead of the bus. They said the bus route was often slow because of traffic delays.

Train travel has some clear advantages. You can book a reservation online and check the departure time on your phone. The fare is sometimes cheaper than a taxi, especially if you buy a weekly ticket. Passengers also know the exact platform before they arrive at the station.

However, not everyone agrees. Some parents worry when there is a long delay in winter. Others think the destination station is too far from the sports centre. The school newspaper asked readers: "Is the train really the best choice?"

Most students answered yes. They explained that they were reading or doing homework while they were travelling. One student wrote, "I will keep using the train next term because it saves time on busy days."
passage.imagePrompt: A modern train station platform with students carrying backpacks, a departure board showing times, and a train arriving.

questions.questionText: What is the main topic of the article?
questions.explanation: Bài so sánh lý do học sinh chọn tàu và ý kiến trái chiều.

choices.text: How to buy a cheaper bus ticket
Whether the train is a good choice for students

questions.questionText: Put the sections in order: (1) Advantages of the train (2) Students switching from bus (3) Different opinions
questions.explanation: Bài: chuyển sang tàu → ưu điểm → ý kiến khác nhau.

exercises.title: Apply: Match Facts from the Article
exercises.instructions: Use what you read. Match each item to the correct fact.

passage.title: Why More Students Take the Train
passage.text:
Last year, many students at Riverside School started taking the train to the city centre instead of the bus. They said the bus route was often slow because of traffic delays.

Train travel has some clear advantages. You can book a reservation online and check the departure time on your phone. The fare is sometimes cheaper than a taxi, especially if you buy a weekly ticket. Passengers also know the exact platform before they arrive at the station.

However, not everyone agrees. Some parents worry when there is a long delay in winter. Others think the destination station is too far from the sports centre. The school newspaper asked readers: "Is the train really the best choice?"

Most students answered yes. They explained that they were reading or doing homework while they were travelling. One student wrote, "I will keep using the train next term because it saves time on busy days."
passage.imagePrompt: A modern train station platform with students carrying backpacks, a departure board showing times, and a train arriving.

questions.questionText: Match each item from the article to the correct fact.
questions.explanation: Mỗi mục khớp với chi tiết trong bài viết.

lessons.title: Lesson 4: Station Announcements
lessons.learningObjective: Understand main ideas in announcements and spell names and places from recordings.

exercises.title: Learn: Platform Change Announcement
exercises.instructions: Listen to the announcement. Answer the questions.

script.title: Platform Change Announcement

lines.speaker: Announcer
lines.text: Good afternoon, passengers. The 4:15 train to Brighton has a fifteen-minute delay.

lines.speaker: Announcer
lines.text: Please go to platform 6, not platform 4. The departure time is now 4:30.

lines.speaker: Announcer
lines.text: Passengers for Oxford should spell their surname at the ticket desk: O-X-F-O-R-D.

lines.speaker: Announcer
lines.text: Thank you. Have a safe journey to your destination.

script.audioNotes: Clear station announcement, moderate pace. Destination names spelled clearly. Approx. 35 seconds.

questions.questionText: What is the announcement mainly about?
questions.explanation: Thông báo về delay và đổi platform cho tàu đi Brighton.

choices.text: A train delay and platform change

questions.questionText: Which platform should passengers go to?
questions.explanation: Please go to platform 6, not platform 4.

exercises.title: Practice: More from the Announcement
exercises.instructions: Listen again to the same announcement. Answer the next questions.

script.title: Platform Change Announcement

lines.speaker: Announcer
lines.text: Good afternoon, passengers. The 4:15 train to Brighton has a fifteen-minute delay.

lines.speaker: Announcer
lines.text: Please go to platform 6, not platform 4. The departure time is now 4:30.

lines.speaker: Announcer
lines.text: Passengers for Oxford should spell their surname at the ticket desk: O-X-F-O-R-D.

lines.speaker: Announcer
lines.text: Thank you. Have a safe journey to your destination.

script.audioNotes: Clear station announcement, moderate pace. Destination names spelled clearly. Approx. 35 seconds.

questions.questionText: Passengers for which place should spell their surname?
questions.explanation: Passengers for Oxford should spell their surname.

questions.questionText: How is the place name spelled in the announcement?
questions.explanation: Spell: O-X-F-O-R-D.

exercises.title: Check: Train Reservation Call
exercises.instructions: Listen to a new conversation. Choose the correct answer.

script.title: Train Reservation Call

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.text: Good morning, City Travel. How can I help you?

lines.speaker: Mai
lines.text: Hello. I'd like to make a reservation for two passengers to Manchester on Saturday.

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.text: The morning fare is eighteen pounds each. Departure is at 9:20 from platform 3.

lines.speaker: Mai
lines.text: Perfect. My destination is Manchester Piccadilly. Can I pay by card?

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.text: Yes. I'll email your ticket. The route takes about two hours with one stop.

script.audioNotes: Friendly phone conversation, clear numbers and place names. Approx. 40 seconds.

questions.questionText: What does Mai want to book?
questions.explanation: Reservation for two passengers to Manchester on Saturday.

choices.text: Two tickets to Manchester on Saturday

questions.questionText: What is the departure time and platform?
questions.explanation: Departure is at 9:20 from platform 3.

questions.questionText: How will Mai pay and receive the ticket?
questions.explanation: Can I pay by card? — Yes. I'll email your ticket.

choices.text: Pay by card; ticket by email
Pay online only at the station

exercises.title: Apply: Match What You Heard
exercises.instructions: Listen again to the platform announcement. Match each item to the correct detail.

script.title: Platform Change Announcement

lines.speaker: Announcer
lines.text: Good afternoon, passengers. The 4:15 train to Brighton has a fifteen-minute delay.

lines.speaker: Announcer
lines.text: Please go to platform 6, not platform 4. The departure time is now 4:30.

lines.speaker: Announcer
lines.text: Passengers for Oxford should spell their surname at the ticket desk: O-X-F-O-R-D.

lines.speaker: Announcer
lines.text: Thank you. Have a safe journey to your destination.

script.audioNotes: Clear station announcement, moderate pace. Destination names spelled clearly. Approx. 35 seconds.

questions.questionText: Match each item from the platform announcement.
questions.explanation: Mỗi mục khớp với chi tiết trong thông báo.

lessons.title: Lesson 5: Message About a Trip
lessons.learningObjective: Write a short message about travel plans using unit vocabulary and future forms.

exercises.title: Learn: Travel Message Phrases
exercises.instructions: Complete each gap with a word from the box: departure, reservation, platform, delay, fare.

questions.questionText: Complete: Hi Tom, | I made a [0] for Saturday. | The [1] is 10:15. | The [2] is eight pounds.
questions.explanation: reservation (đặt chỗ); departure (giờ đi); fare (giá vé).

content.template:
Hi Tom,

I made a [0] for Saturday. The [1] is 10:15. The [2] is eight pounds.

exercises.title: Practice: Build Travel Sentences
exercises.instructions: Put the words in order to make correct sentences.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: I / am going to / take / the train / tomorrow.
questions.explanation: I am going to take the train tomorrow. — going to + plan.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: We / will / meet / at / platform 3.
questions.explanation: We will meet at platform 3. — will + decision.

exercises.title: Check: Message About Your Trip
exercises.instructions: Write a message to a friend. Write at least 25 words.

content.taskDescription: You are going to visit another city at the weekend. Write a short message to your friend Tom. Tell him about your travel plans.
prompts: Say how you will travel and when you are leaving (departure).
Mention one thing you have arranged (reservation, ticket, or meeting).
End the message in a friendly way.
successCriteria: Uses a future form (will, going to, or present continuous)
Uses at least two unit vocabulary words

modelAnswer.text:
Hi Tom,

I am going to visit Manchester this Saturday. My train departure is at 9:20 and I made a reservation online. The fare was eighteen pounds. I will meet my cousin at the station. Are you free on Sunday?

See you,
Mai

grammar.criteria: Uses future forms (will, going to, present continuous) correctly.

vocabulary.criteria: Uses travel words (destination, departure, fare, reservation, platform) appropriately.

organization.criteria: Message has greeting, clear details and a friendly closing.

taskAchievement.criteria: Explains travel plans with destination and departure; at least 25 words.

questions.questionText: Write your response following the task.
questions.explanation: Chấm theo rubric. Bài mẫu cho thấy độ dài và nội dung mong đợi ở A2.

exercises.title: Apply: Complete the Message Frames
exercises.instructions: Complete the message frames with the correct unit words.

questions.questionText: Complete: Dear Ana, | There was a [0] on my route. | I [1] take the bus instead. | My new [2] is 6:40. | Bye, | Linh
questions.explanation: delay (sự trễ); will (quyết định); departure (giờ đi).

content.template:
Dear Ana,

There was a [0] on my route. I [1] take the bus instead. My new [2] is 6:40.

Bye,
Linh

lessons.title: Lesson 6: Talk About Travel
lessons.learningObjective: Answer interview questions about travel using unit vocabulary and grammar.

exercises.title: Learn: Choose the Best Reply
exercises.instructions: Imagine an examiner asks you a question. Choose the best answer.

questions.questionText: Examiner: How do you usually travel to school? You say:
questions.explanation: Trả lời tự nhiên về phương tiện đi học.

choices.distractorNote: Fare is not a verb here

choices.text: I usually take the bus to school.

questions.questionText: Examiner: Have you ever had a long delay? You say:
questions.explanation: Past simple + delay — mô tả chuyến đi bị trễ.

choices.text: Yes, we have a delay every platform.
Yes, we had a two-hour delay last winter.

questions.questionText: Examiner: What are you doing this weekend? You say:
questions.explanation: Present continuous cho sắp xếp: I am visiting...

choices.text: I am visiting my aunt in Brighton.

exercises.title: Practice: Best Response
exercises.instructions: Choose the best phrase you would say in each situation.

questions.questionText: Examiner: Where is your favourite destination? You say:
questions.explanation: Destination + tên địa điểm — cấu trúc phỏng vấn A2.

choices.text: My favourite departure is the coast.
My favourite destination is the coast near Da Nang.
My favourite passenger is the coast.

questions.questionText: Examiner: Are you going to travel by train next month? You say:
questions.explanation: Going to + kế hoạch: Yes, I am going to...

choices.text: Yes, I going to take the train.
Yes, I am going to take the train to Hanoi.
Yes, I will going to take the train.

questions.questionText: Examiner: Tell me about a time you were travelling. You say:
questions.explanation: Past continuous: I was travelling when...

choices.text: I am reading on the train when we stopped.
I was reading on the train when we stopped because of a delay.
I read on the train when we are stopping.

exercises.title: Check: Travel Interview
exercises.instructions: Answer the examiner's questions about how you travel. Speak for up to two minutes.

questions.questionText: Record your answers to the follow-up questions.
questions.explanation: Chấm tổng thể theo bốn tiêu chí. Trả lời ngắn vẫn được điểm một phần nếu dùng đúng từ vựng.

exercises.title: Apply: Situational Response
exercises.instructions: Choose the best thing to say in each travel situation.

questions.questionText: Your friend's train is late. You want to help. You say:
questions.explanation: Đề nghị giúp đỡ lịch sự khi có delay.

choices.text: Don't worry. I am fare with you.
Don't worry. I will wait with you at the platform.
Don't worry. I departure with you.

questions.questionText: The clerk asks about your destination. You say:
questions.explanation: Nêu rõ destination khi đặt vé.

choices.text: My reservation is Manchester Piccadilly, please.
My destination is Manchester Piccadilly, please.
My delay is Manchester Piccadilly, please.

questions.questionText: You are telling a friend about tomorrow's plan. You say:
questions.explanation: Present continuous cho sắp xếp: I am meeting... at the station.

choices.text: I meet my cousin at the station every 5 p.m.
I am meeting my cousin at the station at 5 p.m.
I am meet my cousin at the station.
